description: FR-first luxury funeral florals polish pass for Mossa v3, evolving the live v2 site into a calmer, more refined editorial experience with corrected bottom-button behavior, a new minimal vector logo system, optimized asset categorization, and explicit browser-tested interaction flows for mobile and desktop.

typography.headline: Elegant serif with restrained contrast, oversized spacing
typography.body: Neutral sans with high readability and generous line-height

designSystem.motionPhilosophy: Slow, meditative reveals only. No decorative bounce. Scroll-linked softness, opacity fades, and subtle elevation on hover.
aestheticGoals: Editorial whitespace over dense commerce UI
Client-impressing visual calm on first viewport

bilingualArchitecture.rule: French-first across navigation, CTAs, metadata, alt text, and testing. English remains secondary and non-prominent.

routes.languageMode: FR root-first with optional EN content toggle

structure.appShell: single-page editorial feel with lightweight multi-route support
structure.contentSource: client research/copy JSON normalized into content.ts
structure.mediaSource: clients/mossa/assets with categorized manifest and optimized derivatives
structure.testingSource: browser tool scenarios defined in src/lib/test-scenarios.ts

featureFixes.summary: Any bottom action button or category/filter change that swaps content must force a smooth scroll-to-top of the updated content region or page root.
implementation: Attach to BottomActionBar and CategoryFilterBar state changes
Respect reduced-motion by switching to instant scroll when needed
Prevent stale viewport issue on mobile after content swap
acceptance: Changing any bottom action returns user to top of the refreshed content

featureFixes.summary: Replace imperfect raster-first logo presentation with a smooth, faultless minimal SVG logo mark and wordmark system.
implementation: Use client logo files as reference only, not final rendering source
Ensure clean stroke curves and transparent background rendering
acceptance: No visible artifacting or jagged edges

featureFixes.summary: Organize the 48 client assets into funeral-appropriate categories and optimization priorities.
implementation: Create asset-manifest.ts grouping logo, brand reference, care, and product imagery
Tag product photos by likely use: urne/memorial, naissance, interieur, detail, supporting texture
Identify hero candidates and avoid mismatched cheerful imagery in memorial surfaces
Prepare optimization notes for WebP conversion/cropping without altering originals
acceptance: Builder/Media can source from a single manifest

featureFixes.summary: Define explicit browser-tool verification flow for all major buttons.
implementation: Document snapshot -> act/click -> compare state test cases
Verify route changes, content swaps, scroll resets, and CTA visibility
Include mobile and desktop viewport passes
acceptance: Every primary CTA has a defined expected outcome
Bottom actions verified for scroll-to-top behavior

optimizationRules: Create optimized derivatives for UI use only
Prefer WebP for shipped UI images
Preserve transparent PNG only where logo fidelity requires it
Use consistent aspect ratios per section to maintain zen composition

contentStrategy.voice: Contemplative, refined, non-clinical, non-generic, FR-native Quebec luxury

pagesDetail.purpose: Emotion-first landing experience with strongest visual polish
Curated arrangement browsing with category filters and bottom actions
Brand trust, atelier philosophy, Quebec craftsmanship framing

successSignals: Scroll resets when content context changes
No clipped bottom-sheet or hidden content after action
FR labels remain default on reload

performanceBudget.imageStrategy: Prioritize one hero image, lazy-load supporting galleries, compress product set aggressively
performanceBudget.fontStrategy: Use restrained typography footprint, self-host or minimal variable fonts

content.notes: Normalize FR-first copy into content.ts and keep EN secondary. Preserve funeral dignity and avoid cheerful generic floristry language.

media.notes: Use manifest-driven categorization before UI placement. Prefer memorial-suitable imagery for funeral pathways and reserve softer celebratory imagery for naissance or atelier storytelling.

blueprintNotes.designPattern: Memorial editorial flow — the site behaves like a calm guided passage, where each route transition and filter action restores orientation through automatic scroll reset, preserving dignity and preventing cognitive friction during emotional browsing.
blueprintNotes.templateReasoning: No exact nextjs-ui-polish template existed. Best match was landing-page for editorial marketing structure, augmented with web-app route/data behavior and FR-first luxury constraints from prior Mossa work.
blueprintNotes.clientImpressMove: Introduce a bespoke SVG logo system plus stone-paper texture, measured motion, and carefully cropped memorial imagery so the site feels custom-designed rather than merely polished.
